title: Backlit Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale backlit push-through signs with high-output LED illumination. UL 48 listed, German-engineered, 3-week delivery. Trade-only pricing for sign shops.
heroSubtitle: LED-illuminated push-through letter signs engineered for brilliant nighttime presence and lasting durability. UL 48 listed, built to order for the sign trade.

sections.heading: How LED Backlighting Transforms Push-Through Signs
sections.content:
Backlit push-through signs combine dimensional letterforms with internal LED illumination to create one of the most visually striking sign types available. The construction starts with a CNC-routed aluminum panel — each letter opening precision-cut to tight tolerances — paired with cast acrylic letters that are pushed through from behind so their faces sit proud of the panel surface. Behind the letters, high-output LED modules wash light through the translucent acrylic, producing an even, vibrant glow that commands attention after dark.

At Sunlite Signs, our backlit push-through signs are built on a foundation of German engineering discipline. Every aluminum panel is routed on multi-axis CNC equipment to ensure letter openings match the acrylic forms within ±0.010" tolerance. This precision fit is critical: it eliminates unsightly light leaks between the letter edge and the panel cutout while allowing the letters to seat firmly without adhesive failure over time.

Our LED systems are purpose-selected for push-through applications. We use Samsung or Nichia modules rated at 160+ lumens per watt with a 50,000-hour lifespan, mounted on aluminum heat-sink channels inside the sign cabinet. Paired with UL-recognized Mean Well drivers, these systems deliver consistent brightness from day one through year ten and beyond. Because we sell exclusively to the sign trade, you get wholesale pricing on a product your clients will associate with premium quality.

sections.heading: Engineering Specifications for Backlit Push-Through Signs
sections.content:
Every backlit push-through sign from Sunlite Signs is engineered as a complete system through our complimentary engineering — cabinet, panel, letters, lighting, and power supply working together for maximum performance and serviceability, with all components UL 48 listed. Here's what goes into each unit:

  • Cabinet Construction: Fully welded 0.080" aluminum housing with internal LED mounting rails and ventilation channels. Standard depths of 4", 5", or 6" to accommodate different letter sizes and lighting layouts.
  • Routed Panel: 0.125" aluminum face panel, CNC-routed with letter cutouts. Finished in automotive-grade polyurethane paint in any PMS or RAL color.
  • Acrylic Letters: Cast acrylic in 1/2" or 3/4" thickness, with polished edges for clean light transmission. Letters protrude 1/4" to 3/8" through the face panel for a dimensional effect.
  • LED Layout: Modules are spaced to achieve uniform illumination across each letter, with no hot spots or dark corners. We run photometric calculations on every layout before production.
  • Power Supply: Mean Well constant-voltage drivers, UL recognized, accessible via a rear or bottom service panel for easy maintenance.

All backlit push-through signs carry UL 48 certification covering the complete assembly. Signs ship fully wired, tested, and ready for installation. We include detailed wiring diagrams, mounting templates, and all necessary hardware. Standard lead time is three weeks from approved artwork.

sections.heading: Applications for Backlit Push-Through Letter Signs
sections.content:
Backlit push-through signs are among the most versatile illuminated sign types, suitable for a wide range of commercial applications where nighttime visibility and architectural presence matter. Explore our full push-through signs collection. Their clean, modern aesthetic — an alternative to front-lit channel letters and dimensional depth make them a natural fit for businesses that want to project quality and permanence.

Common applications include:

  • Retail storefronts: National and regional retailers use backlit push-through signage to maintain brand consistency across multiple locations while achieving a high-end look that halo-lit channel letters cannot match.
  • Hotels and hospitality: Push-through signs deliver a refined arrival experience, with the soft glow of backlit letters creating an inviting, upscale feel on building facades and porte-cochères.
  • Restaurants and bars: Evening-dependent businesses benefit enormously from the visibility that backlit push-through letters provide, drawing foot traffic from surrounding blocks.
  • Corporate campuses: Lobby and exterior building identification signs gain visual impact when push-through letters are illuminated from behind, reinforcing brand authority.

Sunlite Signs works directly with sign shops and trade partners to deliver backlit push-through signs that match exacting brand specifications. Whether your client needs a single storefront sign or a rollout across dozens of locations, our production capacity and consistent quality make us the wholesale partner sign professionals trust.

faqs.question: What type of LEDs are used in backlit push-through signs?
faqs.answer: We use high-efficiency Samsung or Nichia LED modules rated at 160+ lumens per watt with a 50,000-hour lifespan. These are mounted on aluminum heat-sink channels and powered by UL-recognized Mean Well drivers for long-term reliability.

faqs.question: Are backlit push-through signs UL listed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every backlit push-through sign we manufacture carries UL 48 certification covering the complete assembly, including the electrical system. This is required for code compliance in the majority of U.S. jurisdictions.

faqs.question: How long does production take for a backlit push-through sign?
faqs.answer: Our standard lead time is three weeks from approved artwork and engineering sign-off. Rush production is available for time-sensitive projects — contact our team for scheduling availability.

faqs.question: Can I specify custom LED color temperatures?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We offer standard options of 6500K, 5000K, and 4000K, along with custom color temperatures and RGB modules for dynamic color-changing effects. Every configuration is designed for even illumination across the letter faces.

faqs.question: Do you sell backlit push-through signs to the public?
faqs.answer: No. Sunlite Signs is strictly a wholesale manufacturer. We sell exclusively to licensed sign companies, resellers, and trade professionals. Your clients stay yours — we never compete with our partners.

title: Acrylic Push-Through Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale cast acrylic push-through letters with polished edges and precision CNC routing. German-engineered quality, UL 48 listed, 3-week lead time for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Premium cast acrylic letters precision-fitted to CNC-routed panels for clean light transmission and lasting dimensional impact. Trade-only wholesale pricing.

sections.heading: Why Cast Acrylic Is the Standard for Push-Through Letters
sections.content:
Cast acrylic is the material of choice for push-through letters because of its superior optical clarity, structural integrity, and long-term weatherability — critical for the LED-illuminated push-through format. Unlike extruded acrylic, which is manufactured by pushing material through a die, cast acrylic is formed in individual molds — a process that yields a denser, more uniform sheet with fewer internal stresses. This matters enormously in push-through sign applications, where the acrylic must transmit LED light evenly while resisting cracking, crazing, and UV degradation over years of outdoor exposure.

At Sunlite Signs, we source our cast acrylic exclusively from tier-one manufacturers including Plexiglas, Acrylite, and Lucite. Every sheet meets or exceeds ASTM D4802 standards for optical quality and weatherability. We CNC-route each letter from solid cast sheet stock — never laser-cut, which can introduce micro-stresses along the cut edge that lead to crazing over time.

The result is a push-through letter with crystal-clear edges that transmit light beautifully when backlit, and a face surface that resists yellowing even after years of direct sun exposure. For sign shops, specifying cast acrylic push-through letters means fewer warranty callbacks and happier end clients — a combination that protects your reputation and your margins.

sections.heading: Fabrication Process and Material Options
sections.content:
Our acrylic push-through letter fabrication process is built around precision and repeatability, backed by complimentary engineering. Each letter begins as a digital file — we accept vector artwork in AI, EPS, or PDF formats, or we can convert your client's logo through our in-house digitizing service. From there, our engineering team nests the letters for optimal material yield and programs the CNC router for the specific acrylic thickness and font geometry.

Key fabrication details include:

  • Thickness Options: 1/4", 3/8", 1/2", and 3/4" cast acrylic. Thicker stock provides more side-glow for dramatic nighttime effects, while thinner options work well for smaller letter heights.
  • Edge Finishing: All letters receive flame-polished or diamond-polished edges for maximum light transmission and a premium appearance.
  • Material Grades: Standard white translucent (most common for backlit applications), clear, frosted, and a full spectrum of colored translucent acrylics. We also offer specialty materials like fluorescent and light-diffusing acrylic.
  • Minimum Letter Height: 2" for standard fonts; complex scripts or thin-stroke typefaces may require larger minimum sizes to ensure structural integrity during push-through installation.

Every letter is test-fitted to its corresponding panel cutout before shipping to verify a precise, friction-fit seat. This attention to detail ensures your installation team spends time hanging the sign — not forcing letters into mismatched openings.

sections.heading: Pairing Acrylic Letters with Routed Panels
sections.content:
The performance of a push-through sign depends entirely on the relationship between the acrylic letter and the routed panel. At Sunlite Signs, we engineer both components as a matched set. Our CNC programs account for material kerf — the same precision routing used for aluminum flat cut letters, thermal expansion coefficients, and the specific interference fit needed to hold the letter securely without relying solely on adhesive.

Panel options include:

  • Flat aluminum panels: 0.125" or 0.188" aluminum sheet, ideal for flush-mount wall installations or integration into existing sign cabinets.
  • Cabinet-mounted panels: The routed face panel is welded or fastened to an aluminum cabinet that houses the LED system and power supply. This is the most common configuration for illuminated push-through signs.
  • Composite panels: ACM (aluminum composite material) panels can be used for non-illuminated applications where weight savings are a priority.

We apply a bead of industrial-grade silicone adhesive around each letter perimeter during final assembly to create a permanent, weather-tight bond. This adhesive is UV-stable and rated for temperatures from -40°F to 300°F, ensuring the letters stay seated through freeze-thaw cycles, thermal expansion, and high-wind events. Every completed assembly is inspected under our quality control protocol before it ships to your shop.

faqs.question: What is the difference between cast and extruded acrylic for push-through letters?
faqs.answer: Cast acrylic is formed in molds, resulting in a denser, more optically uniform material with better resistance to crazing and UV degradation. Extruded acrylic is cheaper but prone to stress cracking and edge crazing, making it unsuitable for push-through sign applications.

faqs.question: What acrylic thickness do you recommend for push-through letters?
faqs.answer: For most applications, we recommend 1/2" cast acrylic. It provides excellent side-glow for backlit signs while maintaining structural integrity during installation. Larger letters or those requiring more dramatic depth may benefit from 3/4" stock.

faqs.question: Can you match specific brand colors in acrylic?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer a full spectrum of colored translucent acrylics from major manufacturers. For precise brand matching, we can source custom-colored sheet stock or apply surface-applied color films to achieve the exact PMS value your client requires.

faqs.question: What is the minimum letter size for acrylic push-through letters?
faqs.answer: Our standard minimum is 2" letter height for most sans-serif fonts. Script fonts, thin-stroke typefaces, or designs with fine detail may require larger minimum sizes to ensure the letters are structurally sound during push-through installation.

faqs.question: How are acrylic push-through letters secured in the panel?
faqs.answer: Letters are precision-fitted with a slight interference fit and bonded with industrial-grade UV-stable silicone adhesive rated from -40°F to 300°F. This combination of mechanical fit and chemical bond ensures letters stay seated through all weather conditions.

title: Aluminum Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale aluminum push-through signs with CNC-routed panels and precision-fitted acrylic letters. German-engineered, UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: CNC-routed aluminum panels paired with dimensional acrylic letters for signs that combine structural strength with striking visual depth. Built for the trade.

sections.heading: The Role of Aluminum in Push-Through Sign Construction
sections.content:
Aluminum is the backbone of every push-through sign, just as it is for aluminum channel letters. It serves as the structural panel through which acrylic letters are pushed, the cabinet housing that contains the LED system, and often the mounting framework that secures the assembly to the building — all covered by our complimentary engineering. Its combination of light weight, corrosion resistance, and machinability makes it uniquely suited for this application — no other commercially available metal delivers the same balance of properties at a comparable cost.

At Sunlite Signs, we work exclusively with 5052-H32 and 6061-T6 aluminum alloys for our push-through sign panels. The 5052-H32 alloy offers superior corrosion resistance and is our standard choice for coastal installations, while 6061-T6 provides higher tensile strength for signs that must span large unsupported distances or resist significant wind loads.

Our CNC routing process removes material from the aluminum panel to create the letter-shaped openings through which acrylic forms are inserted. We program each routing operation to account for the specific alloy's machining characteristics, tool wear patterns, and the thermal behavior of the panel during cutting. The result is a panel with burr-free, dimensionally precise cutouts that accept the acrylic letters with a clean friction fit — no hand-finishing required.

sections.heading: CNC Routing Process and Panel Specifications
sections.content:
Precision CNC routing is what separates a professional-grade push-through sign from a subpar one. At Sunlite Signs, our routing operation is managed by a team of experienced CNC programmers who understand the nuances of cutting aluminum — the same expertise behind our aluminum flat cut letters for sign applications.

Our standard panel specifications include:

  • Panel Thickness: 0.125" (1/8") for signs up to 48" wide; 0.188" (3/16") for larger panels or high-wind installations. Custom gauges available on request.
  • Maximum Panel Size: 60" x 144" single-piece panels. Larger signs are fabricated with seamless spliced panels using internal backing plates.
  • Routing Tolerance: ±0.010" on all letter cutouts, verified by digital inspection before letters are test-fitted.
  • Surface Finish: Panels are sanded, primed, and painted with automotive-grade polyurethane in any PMS, RAL, or custom color. Metallic, matte, satin, and gloss finishes available.

For illuminated push-through signs, the routed panel is integrated into a welded aluminum cabinet. Our standard cabinet depths are 4", 5", and 6", with custom depths available for signs that require specific LED throw distances or deeper letter protrusion. All cabinets feature internal mounting rails for LED modules, wiring channels for clean cable management, and access panels for post-installation servicing.

Every panel is serialized and tracked through our production system, so we maintain full traceability from raw material through finished assembly. This level of process control is part of our German engineering heritage and ensures consistent quality order after order.

sections.heading: Durability, Finishing, and Weather Performance
sections.content:
Aluminum push-through signs are built to withstand decades of outdoor exposure with minimal maintenance — similar to our illuminated cabinet signs. The inherent corrosion resistance of aluminum alloys, combined with our multi-layer finishing process, creates a sign surface that resists fading. Our structural engineering ensures wind-load compliance throughout the sign's service life, chalking, and oxidation in even the most demanding environments.

Our finishing process includes:

  • Surface Preparation: Panels are cleaned, etched with chromate conversion coating, and primed with a high-adhesion epoxy primer.
  • Paint System: Two coats of automotive-grade polyurethane paint, followed by a UV-resistant clear coat. This system is rated for 10+ years of outdoor exposure without significant color shift.
  • Edge Treatment: All routed edges are deburred and sealed to prevent moisture ingress at the letter-to-panel interface.

For coastal and high-humidity environments, we offer an upgraded marine-grade finishing package that includes additional primer coats and a thicker clear-coat layer. We have supplied push-through signs to locations throughout the Gulf Coast, Pacific Northwest, and Caribbean without premature finish failure.

Structurally, our aluminum panels and cabinets are engineered to meet IBC wind-load requirements for the installation location. We provide stamped engineering drawings upon request for jurisdictions that require them during the permitting process. Sign shops can count on a product that is not only visually impressive but structurally sound for the long term.

faqs.question: What aluminum alloy do you use for push-through sign panels?
faqs.answer: We use 5052-H32 aluminum as our standard alloy for its excellent corrosion resistance, and 6061-T6 for applications requiring higher structural strength. Both alloys machine cleanly on our CNC routers for precise letter cutouts.

faqs.question: What is the maximum panel size for an aluminum push-through sign?
faqs.answer: Our single-piece panels can be up to 60" x 144". For larger signs, we fabricate seamless spliced panels using internal backing plates that are invisible from the viewing side.

faqs.question: How tight are the routing tolerances on letter cutouts?
faqs.answer: We maintain ±0.010" tolerance on all letter cutouts, verified by digital inspection. This precision ensures acrylic letters seat cleanly with minimal light leak at the panel interface.

faqs.question: Can aluminum push-through sign panels be painted in custom colors?
faqs.answer: Yes. We apply automotive-grade polyurethane paint in any PMS, RAL, or custom color. Metallic, matte, satin, and gloss finishes are all available, with a UV-resistant clear coat for 10+ years of outdoor durability.

faqs.question: Do you offer engineering stamps for permit applications?
faqs.answer: We provide stamped engineering drawings upon request for jurisdictions that require them. Our structural calculations account for sign size, mounting method, substrate type, and local wind-load requirements per IBC standards.

title: Restaurant Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale push-through signs for restaurants and hospitality. Backlit dimensional letters, custom fonts, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for sign trade professionals.
heroSubtitle: Dimensional backlit signage that elevates restaurant and hospitality brands. Custom typefaces, premium finishes, and the nighttime presence diners notice.

sections.heading: Why Restaurants Choose Push-Through Signs
sections.content:
The restaurant and hospitality industry demands signage that does more than identify a location — it needs to set a mood, signal quality, and draw guests in from the street. For lettering-only alternatives, consider front-lit channel letters or illuminated blade signs. Push-through signs accomplish all three objectives with a combination of dimensional depth, material richness, and controlled illumination that flat signage simply cannot replicate.

A push-through sign presents a restaurant's name or logo as sculpted acrylic letterforms protruding through a precision-routed metal panel. During daylight hours, the dimensional letters cast natural shadows that create visual texture and depth. After dark, internal LEDs illuminate the translucent acrylic from behind, transforming the sign into a glowing beacon that is visible from blocks away. This day-to-night versatility is exactly what restaurant operators need in competitive dining districts.

Sunlite Signs has manufactured push-through signage for fast-casual chains, fine-dining establishments, boutique hotels, craft breweries, and everything in between. We understand that hospitality branding often involves custom typography, specific color palettes, and design elements that must translate perfectly from a designer's screen to a three-dimensional sign face. Our engineering team works directly with your shop to ensure every curve, serif, and ligature is faithfully reproduced at the scale and thickness your client's design demands.

sections.heading: Design Considerations for Hospitality Signage
sections.content:
Restaurant and hospitality push-through signs present unique design challenges that require experienced fabrication. Script fonts, decorative serifs, and logo elements with fine detail are common in this sector — and they demand careful LED module placement and engineering to ensure structural integrity and clean illumination.

Key design factors we address on every hospitality project:

  • Typography: Script and decorative fonts often have thin strokes, tight counters, and connecting ligatures that must be reinforced or slightly modified for push-through fabrication. Our design team identifies potential weak points and recommends minimum stroke widths — typically 3/8" minimum for 1/2" thick acrylic — to prevent breakage during installation and thermal cycling.
  • Color and Finish: Restaurant brands frequently specify rich, specific colors. We match any PMS value on the aluminum panel and offer the full range of translucent colored acrylics for the push-through letters, including warm ambers, deep reds, and custom tints that complement the restaurant's interior palette.
  • Scale and Proportion: Push-through signs for restaurants are often mounted at eye level or just above awning height, meaning every detail is visible at close range. We pay particular attention to letter spacing (kerning), edge quality, and paint finish at these viewing distances.
  • Dual Branding: Many hospitality projects include both the primary name and a tagline, cuisine type, or secondary logo element. We design multi-line push-through layouts with appropriate hierarchy and spacing.

We provide full-color renderings and shop drawings for approval before production begins, so your client knows exactly what the finished sign will look like on their facade.

sections.heading: Installation Scenarios for Restaurant Signage
sections.content:
Restaurant push-through signs are installed in a wide variety of architectural contexts, from historic brick storefronts to modern glass-and-steel mixed-use developments. Sunlite Signs engineers every sign for the specific installation scenario, accounting for substrate type, structural capacity, and local code requirements.

Common installation configurations include:

  • Above-entrance wall mount: The most traditional placement, with the sign centered above the main entry. We provide concealed mounting brackets that keep the hardware invisible from the street.
  • Under-canopy mount: For restaurants with awnings or covered walkways, push-through signs can be suspended below the canopy for close-range pedestrian visibility. Double-face versions are available for readability from both directions.
  • Multi-tenant installations: Strip mall and food hall environments often require coordinated sign bands. We can produce push-through signs sized to specific landlord criteria while maintaining each restaurant's unique brand identity.
  • Historic district compliance: Many restaurant locations fall within historic preservation zones that limit sign size, illumination, and materials. Our team is experienced with these requirements and can design compliant push-through signs that still deliver strong visual impact.

Every sign ships with detailed installation documentation, including mounting templates, wiring diagrams, and load calculations. For multi-location restaurant rollouts, we maintain artwork and engineering files on record so reorders are fast and consistent. Sign shops working on hospitality programs appreciate our ability to produce identical signs for dozens of locations without quality variation.

faqs.question: Can you produce push-through signs with script or decorative restaurant fonts?
faqs.answer: Yes. We regularly fabricate push-through signs with script, serif, and decorative typefaces. Our engineering team analyzes thin strokes and connecting elements to ensure structural integrity, recommending minimum stroke widths based on acrylic thickness and letter height.

faqs.question: What color options are available for restaurant push-through signs?
faqs.answer: The aluminum panel can be painted in any PMS, RAL, or custom color. Push-through letters are available in white translucent, clear, frosted, or a full spectrum of colored translucent acrylics to match your client's brand palette.

faqs.question: Are push-through signs suitable for historic restaurant districts?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We regularly design push-through signs for historic preservation zones. Our team works within size, illumination, and material restrictions while maximizing visual impact. Non-illuminated configurations are also available where lit signs are prohibited.

faqs.question: Can you support multi-location restaurant sign rollouts?
faqs.answer: Yes. We maintain artwork and engineering files on record for rollout programs, ensuring identical quality across every location. Our production capacity handles large-volume orders with consistent three-week lead times per batch.

faqs.question: Do restaurant push-through signs work for under-canopy installations?
faqs.answer: Yes. We manufacture both single-face and double-face push-through signs suitable for under-canopy and suspended mounting. Double-face versions provide visibility from both pedestrian directions, which is ideal for covered walkways and awning installations.

title: Retail Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale push-through signs for retail storefronts. Dimensional backlit letters, brand-consistent finishes, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for sign trade only.
heroSubtitle: Storefront push-through signage that elevates retail brands with dimensional letters and brilliant backlighting. Engineered for consistency across multi-location programs.

sections.heading: Push-Through Signs as a Retail Branding Tool
sections.content:
In competitive retail corridors, the storefront sign is often the first and most important brand touchpoint a potential customer encounters. Push-through signs give retailers a significant advantage over flat-panel, LED light box or vinyl-on-raceway alternatives by adding genuine three-dimensional depth to the brand presentation. The acrylic letters projecting through the aluminum panel create real shadows, real texture, and a sense of permanence that signals to shoppers that this is an established, quality-focused business.

For national and regional retail chains, push-through signs offer another critical advantage: consistency. Because the sign is manufactured as a precision-engineered assembly — CNC-routed panel matched to CNC-cut acrylic letters — the result is identical from unit to unit. Whether you're outfitting three locations or three hundred, every sign will have the same letter spacing, the same depth, the same paint match, and the same light output. This level of repeatability is essential for brand standards compliance.

Sunlite Signs is the wholesale manufacturing partner sign shops turn to for retail push-through programs. We produce signs for well-known retail brands across fashion, sporting goods, electronics, home furnishings, and specialty retail — always through our trade partners, never direct to the end user. Our commitment to wholesale-only distribution means your client relationship is protected, your margins are preserved, and the quality is consistently excellent.

sections.heading: Specifications for Retail Storefront Applications
sections.content:
Retail push-through signs must meet demanding requirements for visibility, durability, and brand accuracy. Sunlite Signs engineers every retail sign to perform in real-world storefront conditions using commercial-grade LED modules, where the sign may face direct sun exposure, temperature extremes, wind loads, and the constant vibration of urban environments.

Standard specifications for retail push-through signs include:

  • Letter Heights: Typical retail applications range from 12" to 36" letter heights, though we produce letters from 2" to 60"+ for specialized applications.
  • Panel Dimensions: Standard panels up to 60" x 144" as single pieces. Multi-panel assemblies with concealed splices for longer sign runs.
  • Illumination: High-output LED modules in 5000K or 6500K for maximum daytime and nighttime visibility. Consistent color rendering across all units in a rollout program.
  • Mounting Systems: Concealed cleat-mount, through-bolt, or z-clip systems depending on the substrate and landlord requirements. We work with your shop to match the mounting method to the specific installation conditions.

Retail landlords and property managers often impose strict sign criteria governing size, projection depth, electrical connections, and finish quality. Our engineering team reviews tenant sign criteria documents as part of our standard process, ensuring the sign we produce will pass landlord approval the first time. This saves your shop time, avoids costly remakes, and keeps the project on schedule.

For multi-location programs, we assign a dedicated project manager who coordinates production schedules, manages artwork consistency, and ensures that every sign in the series meets the same exacting standards.

sections.heading: Multi-Location Rollouts and Brand Consistency
sections.content:
Managing a retail sign rollout across multiple locations is one of the most demanding projects a sign shop can undertake — our complimentary engineering streamlines the process. Every location has different facade dimensions, different substrates, different landlord requirements — yet the end result must look identical from store to store. Sunlite Signs has the production systems and project management infrastructure to make these programs run smoothly.

Our rollout process includes:

  • Master Artwork File: We create a single approved master file that serves as the template for all locations. Any site-specific modifications (size adjustments, mounting changes) are documented as variants of the master, ensuring brand consistency while accommodating field conditions.
  • Batch Production: Signs are manufactured in coordinated batches timed to your installation schedule. We can produce and hold inventory for phased rollouts, shipping to each location as your crew is ready to install.
  • Quality Documentation: Every sign is photographed before shipping, with dimensional verification records maintained for the entire program. This documentation provides an audit trail that satisfies brand managers and property owners.
  • Replacement Parts: We maintain CAM files and material records for every rollout program, so replacement letters, panels, or LED modules can be produced quickly years after the original installation.

Sign shops that partner with Sunlite Signs on retail rollouts benefit from predictable pricing, consistent lead times, and a manufacturing partner that treats every unit in the series with the same attention to detail as a one-off custom sign. Our capacity allows us to handle programs ranging from a handful of locations to nationwide deployments without compromising quality or delivery timelines.

faqs.question: Can you match specific retail brand standards for push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. We work from brand standards documents to match exact PMS colors, letter proportions, spacing, and finish specifications. Our quality control process includes side-by-side color verification and dimensional inspection against the approved master artwork.

faqs.question: How do you ensure consistency across a multi-location retail rollout?
faqs.answer: We create a master artwork file and produce signs in coordinated batches using the same material lots and paint batches whenever possible. Every sign is photographed and dimensionally verified before shipping to ensure uniformity across the program.

faqs.question: What mounting options are available for retail storefront push-through signs?
faqs.answer: We offer concealed cleat-mount, through-bolt, and z-clip systems. Our engineering team reviews landlord sign criteria and installation conditions to recommend the appropriate mounting method for each location.

faqs.question: Can you work with landlord sign criteria documents?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Reviewing tenant sign criteria is part of our standard process. We ensure the sign meets all landlord requirements for size, projection depth, electrical connections, and finish before production begins.

faqs.question: Do you offer replacement parts for previously installed retail signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. We maintain CAM files and material records for every project. Replacement letters, panels, or LED modules can be produced quickly, even years after the original installation, ensuring your clients' signs stay looking new.

title: Custom Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale custom push-through signs with bespoke fonts, logos, and design elements. German-engineered precision, UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery for sign professionals.
heroSubtitle: From custom typefaces to intricate logo elements, we engineer bespoke push-through signs that bring your clients' unique brand visions to life in dimensional acrylic and aluminum.

sections.heading: Bringing Custom Designs to Life in Push-Through Format
sections.content:
Every business has a unique brand identity, and the most impactful signage reflects that individuality through custom design elements. Custom push-through signs take the inherent visual power — rivaling custom shaped logo signs for brand impact of dimensional backlit letters and extend it to any typeface, logo, graphic element, or decorative motif your client can imagine. At Sunlite Signs, custom work is not a special request — it is the core of what we do.

Our engineering and design team translates two-dimensional artwork into three-dimensional push-through sign components every day. This translation process is where our German engineering heritage shows its value. Converting a logo with variable stroke widths, overlapping elements, or complex curves into a set of CNC-routable acrylic forms and a matching routed panel requires deep understanding of material behavior, structural limits, and optical properties.

We evaluate every custom design against a set of fabrication criteria: minimum stroke width relative to acrylic thickness, minimum counter size (the enclosed spaces within letters like O, B, and R), connection points on script and ligature fonts, and aspect ratios that affect structural integrity under wind load and thermal cycling. When a design element falls outside optimal parameters, we propose engineering solutions — subtle stroke modifications, internal reinforcement tabs, or strategic breakpoints — that preserve the design intent while ensuring the sign performs reliably for years.

sections.heading: Custom Typography and Logo Engineering
sections.content:
Typography is where custom push-through signs truly differentiate themselves. While standard channel letter signs can accommodate most fonts without modification, push-through signs present unique structural challenges because the acrylic letter must pass through a close-tolerance opening in the aluminum panel and remain securely seated under all environmental conditions.

Our custom font engineering process includes:

  • Stroke Analysis: We map every stroke in the typeface to identify areas below our minimum width threshold. For 1/2" acrylic, we recommend a minimum stroke width of 3/8"; for 3/4" acrylic, the minimum increases to 1/2". Strokes below these minimums are reinforced or slightly thickened with the client's approval.
  • Counter Optimization: Small enclosed counters (like the interior of a lowercase "e" or "a") must be large enough for the CNC router to cleanly cut both the acrylic letter and the panel opening. We calculate minimum counter dimensions based on router bit diameter and material thickness.
  • Connection Engineering: Script fonts and connected letterforms require careful planning at each connection point. We determine where to fabricate letters as single units, where to break them into separate pieces with flush joints, and where to add concealed aluminum backing tabs for additional support.
  • Logo Integration: Non-text elements — icons, symbols, graphic devices — are engineered as push-through components using the same principles. Complex shapes with varying widths may be produced as multiple nested acrylic pieces that assemble into a single visual element.

We provide detailed engineering drawings showing all modifications before production, ensuring your client approves every design decision.

sections.heading: The Custom Order Process from Concept to Delivery
sections.content:
Ordering custom push-through signs from Sunlite Signs follows a structured process designed to minimize revisions, prevent fabrication issues — backed by complimentary engineering, and deliver the finished sign on time and on spec. Here is how a typical custom project flows:

Step 1 — Artwork Submission: Your shop submits vector artwork (AI, EPS, or PDF) along with the desired sign dimensions, letter thickness, color specifications, and installation details. If you have the client's brand guidelines, include those as well.

Step 2 — Engineering Review: Our team evaluates the artwork for push-through feasibility, identifies any areas that need modification, and prepares a preliminary shop drawing showing letter dimensions, panel layout, and recommended acrylic thickness. This review typically takes one to two business days.

Step 3 — Approval and Pricing: We present the shop drawing and detailed quote to your team. The quote includes all components: routed panel, acrylic letters, LED system (if illuminated), paint finish, and hardware. Pricing is fixed once approved — no surprise upcharges.

Step 4 — Production: Upon approval, the sign enters our three-week production queue. CNC routing, acrylic fabrication, painting, LED installation, and assembly all happen under one roof. We test every illuminated sign at full power before it leaves the facility.

Step 5 — Quality Control and Shipping: Each custom sign undergoes a multi-point inspection covering dimensional accuracy, paint quality, LED function, and assembly integrity. Signs are crated in custom packaging designed to prevent transit damage. We ship via common carrier with full insurance and tracking.

For sign shops that handle design in-house, we also offer a design-assist service where our team can help develop the push-through sign concept from initial sketches or rough ideas. This collaborative approach is especially valuable for shops that are new to push-through sign fabrication and want to ensure their first project is a success.

faqs.question: Can any font be used for custom push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Most fonts can be adapted for push-through fabrication. Fonts with very thin strokes or extremely small counters may require minor engineering modifications to ensure structural integrity. We review every design and propose solutions that preserve the original aesthetic.

faqs.question: How do you handle logos with complex shapes in push-through format?
faqs.answer: Complex logos are broken down into individual push-through components, each engineered for structural integrity and clean illumination. Overlapping elements may be fabricated at different depths or with complementary color layers to maintain the design's visual hierarchy.

faqs.question: What is the turnaround time for custom push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Standard production is three weeks from approved artwork and engineering sign-off. The engineering review and approval process typically takes one to two additional business days. Rush production is available for time-critical projects.

faqs.question: Do you offer design-assist services for custom push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our team can help develop push-through sign concepts from initial sketches, rough ideas, or brand guidelines. This collaborative service is especially valuable for shops exploring push-through fabrication for the first time.

faqs.question: Are custom push-through signs more expensive than standard configurations?
faqs.answer: Custom designs may involve additional engineering time, but our pricing remains competitive because we use the same CNC routing and fabrication processes for all push-through signs. We provide fixed quotes upfront with no surprise upcharges.

title: Outdoor Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale outdoor push-through signs engineered for extreme weather. UV-stable acrylic, sealed aluminum cabinets, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for the sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Weatherproof push-through signs built to deliver years of reliable performance in rain, snow, sun, and coastal salt air. German-engineered for the toughest exterior conditions.

sections.heading: Engineering Push-Through Signs for Outdoor Environments
sections.content:
Outdoor push-through signs face every environmental challenge nature can deliver: UV radiation that degrades materials, thermal cycling that stresses joints and adhesives, wind loads that test structural connections, rain and humidity that seek out every unsealed gap, and in coastal locations, salt air that aggressively corrodes unprotected metals. A sign that performs beautifully indoors can fail catastrophically outdoors if it is not specifically engineered for exterior exposure.

At Sunlite Signs, every outdoor push-through sign is designed from the ground up for weather resistance. This is not a matter of applying a waterproof coating to a standard indoor sign — it requires a fundamentally different approach to material selection, joint design, electrical enclosure, and finishing. Our German engineering methodology means we anticipate failure modes before they occur and design them out of the product.

Our outdoor push-through signs have been installed in environments ranging from the desert Southwest (where surface temperatures can exceed 160°F) to northern Minnesota (where winter temperatures drop below -30°F) to coastal Florida (where salt air and hurricane-force winds are regular concerns). In every case, our signs continue to perform because the engineering accounts for the specific environmental demands of the installation location.

sections.heading: Weatherproofing Materials and Construction Details
sections.content:
Building a push-through sign that survives outdoors for a decade or more requires attention to detail at every junction, seam, and connection point. Here is how our wholesale manufacturing team approaches weatherproofing at the component level:

  • Aluminum Cabinet: Fully welded construction with continuous seam welds — no rivets or mechanical fasteners that could create moisture pathways. Internal drainage channels direct any condensation to weep holes at the cabinet base, preventing water accumulation around electrical components.
  • Panel-to-Cabinet Seal: The routed face panel mates to the cabinet with a continuous gasket of closed-cell EPDM foam, creating a compression seal that blocks water infiltration while allowing for thermal expansion.
  • Letter-to-Panel Seal: Each acrylic letter is bonded to the routed panel with UV-stable silicone adhesive rated for -40°F to 300°F. The adhesive bead is applied around the full perimeter of the letter, creating a water-tight bond that flexes with thermal movement without cracking or delaminating.
  • Electrical Enclosure: LED modules and drivers are mounted inside the sealed cabinet on elevated rails to keep them above any condensation that may accumulate. All wiring connections use waterproof connectors rated to IP67 standards. Power entry points are sealed with cord grips and silicone.
  • Paint System: Exterior panels receive our enhanced outdoor finishing process: chromate conversion primer, two coats of polyurethane color, and a UV-blocking clear coat rated for 10+ years of outdoor exposure per ASTM accelerated weathering tests.

This comprehensive approach to weatherproofing means sign shops can confidently specify Sunlite push-through signs for even the most exposed outdoor locations.

sections.heading: UV Resistance and Long-Term Outdoor Performance
sections.content:
Ultraviolet radiation is the silent destroyer of outdoor signage — a concern shared with illuminated cabinet signs and logo boxes. It breaks down polymer chains in plastics, fades pigments in paints, and degrades adhesives — all slowly enough that the damage may not be obvious until the sign has been in service for several years. Push-through signs are particularly vulnerable because the acrylic letters are the primary visual element, and any yellowing, crazing, or loss of clarity is immediately noticeable.

Sunlite Signs addresses UV degradation through material selection and specification:

  • Cast Acrylic: We use only UV-stabilized cast acrylic from manufacturers whose products carry documented UV resistance ratings. Our standard white translucent acrylic maintains greater than 92% light transmission after 10 years of simulated outdoor exposure in ASTM G154 accelerated weathering tests.
  • Paint System: Our automotive-grade polyurethane paint includes UV-absorbing additives in both the color coat and clear coat layers. Field-proven performance across installations dating back over a decade confirms minimal color shift even in high-UV environments like Arizona and southern California.
  • LED Longevity: Our Samsung and Nichia LED modules are rated for 50,000+ hours at L70 (the point where output drops to 70% of initial brightness). In a typical dusk-to-dawn operation cycle, this translates to approximately 12-15 years of service before a module replacement would be recommended.

We also design all outdoor push-through signs with serviceable access panels so that LED modules, drivers, or individual acrylic letters can be replaced in the field without removing the entire sign from the wall. This serviceability extends the effective lifespan of the sign well beyond the life of any individual component — a key selling point for your end clients and their long-term budget planning.

faqs.question: How do you seal push-through signs against water infiltration?
faqs.answer: We use a multi-barrier approach: fully welded cabinet construction, EPDM gaskets at the panel-to-cabinet joint, UV-stable silicone adhesive around every letter perimeter, IP67-rated electrical connectors, and internal drainage channels with weep holes. Every potential moisture pathway is sealed or managed.

faqs.question: What temperature range can outdoor push-through signs withstand?
faqs.answer: Our signs are engineered for continuous outdoor service from -40°F to 130°F ambient temperature. The silicone adhesive that bonds letters to panels is rated from -40°F to 300°F, and all materials are selected for performance across extreme thermal cycling.

faqs.question: Will the acrylic letters yellow over time in outdoor installations?
faqs.answer: We use UV-stabilized cast acrylic that maintains greater than 92% light transmission after 10 years of accelerated weathering testing. This material resists yellowing and crazing far better than extruded acrylic or polycarbonate alternatives.

faqs.question: Can outdoor push-through signs withstand hurricane-force winds?
faqs.answer: Yes, when properly engineered for the installation location. We calculate wind loads per IBC standards and can provide stamped engineering drawings for high-wind zones. Our cabinet construction and mounting systems are designed to meet the structural requirements of the specific project site.

faqs.question: How long do LEDs last in outdoor push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Our Samsung and Nichia LED modules are rated at 50,000+ hours at L70, which translates to approximately 12-15 years of dusk-to-dawn operation before replacement is recommended. All signs include serviceable access panels for easy LED replacement in the field.

title: Large Push-Through Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale large push-through letters up to 60"+ in height. Reinforced acrylic, heavy-duty panels, engineered for visibility and structural integrity. Trade-only pricing.
heroSubtitle: Oversized push-through lettering engineered for maximum visibility on large facades, monuments, and high-rise buildings. Built to scale without compromising quality.

sections.heading: Scaling Push-Through Letters for Maximum Impact
sections.content:
When a building facade spans 100 feet or a monument sign sits 200 feet from the road, standard-sized letters simply disappear. For alternative large-format options, see illuminated cabinet signs. Large push-through letters — typically defined as letter heights of 24" and above, up to 60" or more — solve this visibility challenge while delivering the dimensional depth and backlit presence that make push-through signs so compelling.

Scaling push-through letters is not simply a matter of enlarging the artwork. Larger letters introduce engineering challenges that do not exist at smaller scales: increased acrylic mass requires stronger panel cutout tolerances to prevent sagging, larger surface areas demand more sophisticated LED layouts to achieve uniform illumination, and the structural loads on mounting systems multiply significantly with size. Each of these challenges requires specific engineering solutions.

Sunlite Signs has extensive experience manufacturing large-format push-through letters for commercial buildings, shopping centers, hospitals, universities, and corporate campuses. Our engineering team understands the physics of scaling — how material properties, structural forces, and optical requirements change as letter dimensions increase — and applies this knowledge to every oversized project. The result is a large push-through sign that looks every bit as refined and precisely crafted as its smaller counterpart.

sections.heading: Structural Engineering for Oversized Letters
sections.content:
The primary engineering challenge with large push-through letters is structural. A 48" tall acrylic letter can weigh several pounds, and the panel opening through which it passes must support that weight under all conditions — including wind uplift, thermal expansion, and the vibration of a building facade in gusty conditions. Our approach to structural integrity at scale includes several key strategies:

  • Increased Acrylic Thickness: Large letters are fabricated from 3/4" or 1" cast acrylic to provide the rigidity needed to resist deflection. For letters exceeding 36" in height, we evaluate each design individually and may recommend internal aluminum reinforcement channels bonded to the back of the acrylic to prevent bowing.
  • Heavy-Gauge Panels: Standard 0.125" panels are insufficient for large letters. We upgrade to 0.188" or 0.250" aluminum panels to provide the stiffness needed to support heavy acrylic forms and resist panel flex under wind load.
  • Reinforced Cabinet Construction: Large push-through sign cabinets incorporate internal cross-bracing and structural ribs welded at calculated intervals. These stiffeners prevent the cabinet from racking or deforming under the combined weight of the sign components and external forces.
  • Segmented Letter Construction: Letters exceeding approximately 48" in height may be fabricated in two or more sections with precision-aligned splice joints. The joints are reinforced with internal aluminum backing plates and sealed with color-matched adhesive to create an invisible seam.

Every large push-through letter project includes a structural engineering review that accounts for sign weight, wind load per ASCE 7 standards, mounting substrate capacity, and seismic considerations where applicable. We provide stamped engineering drawings for permit applications upon request.

sections.heading: LED Illumination for Large-Format Push-Through Signs
sections.content:
Achieving uniform illumination across large push-through letters is a sophisticated LED lighting design challenge. As letter dimensions increase, the internal cabinet depth required for even LED wash increases proportionally, and the number and placement of LED modules must be carefully calculated to avoid hot spots near the modules and dark zones at the letter extremities.

Sunlite Signs approaches large-format illumination with the same rigor we apply to structural engineering:

  • Cabinet Depth Optimization: We calculate the minimum cabinet depth required for full letter coverage based on the LED module's beam angle and the letter dimensions. Larger letters typically require 6" to 10" cabinet depths to allow sufficient light spread.
  • Module Layout: LED modules are positioned in a calculated grid pattern that accounts for the specific geometry of each letter. Areas with narrow strokes receive closer module spacing, while wider areas use standard spacing — ensuring uniform brightness across the entire letter face.
  • Reflective Interior: Cabinet interiors are painted with high-reflectance white paint to maximize light recycling and improve overall efficiency. This is especially important in deep cabinets where light must travel further to reach the acrylic face.
  • Power Distribution: Large push-through signs may require multiple LED drivers wired in parallel to handle the total module count. We use a distributed power architecture that prevents a single driver failure from darkening the entire sign — if one circuit fails, only a portion of the sign is affected, and repair is straightforward.

We run photometric simulations on every large-format push-through sign before production begins. These simulations model the light distribution inside the cabinet and predict the visible brightness pattern on the letter faces, allowing us to optimize module placement before cutting a single piece of aluminum. The result is a sign that glows with perfectly even illumination — no hot spots, no dark edges — even at letter heights of four feet and beyond.

faqs.question: What is the maximum letter height for push-through signs?
faqs.answer: We regularly produce push-through letters up to 60" in height and can engineer solutions for even larger formats. Letters exceeding 48" may be fabricated in precision-aligned sections with invisible splice joints for structural integrity.

faqs.question: How do you prevent large acrylic letters from sagging or bowing?
faqs.answer: We increase acrylic thickness to 3/4" or 1" for large letters and bond internal aluminum reinforcement channels to the back of letters exceeding 36" in height. Heavy-gauge panels (0.188" or 0.250") provide additional support at the panel cutout.

faqs.question: What cabinet depth is needed for large push-through letters?
faqs.answer: Large-format push-through signs typically require cabinet depths of 6" to 10" to achieve uniform LED illumination. We calculate the optimal depth based on the LED beam angle, letter dimensions, and desired brightness uniformity.

faqs.question: Can large push-through signs be installed on high-rise buildings?
faqs.answer: Yes. We engineer signs for high-rise applications with full structural analysis including wind load per ASCE 7, seismic considerations, and mounting substrate capacity. Stamped engineering drawings are available for permit applications.

faqs.question: How do you ensure even illumination on very large letters?
faqs.answer: We run photometric simulations for every large-format project to optimize LED module placement. Combined with reflective cabinet interiors and distributed power architecture, this process ensures uniform brightness across the entire letter face without hot spots or dark zones.

secondaryKeywords: sign cabinet with push-through letters wholesale
title: Cabinet Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale cabinet push-through sign combinations with integrated LED illumination. Welded aluminum construction, UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery for sign trade only.
heroSubtitle: Fully integrated sign cabinets with CNC-routed faces and push-through acrylic letters. One assembly, complete illumination, maximum visual impact.

sections.heading: The Integrated Cabinet Push-Through Sign System
sections.content:
A cabinet push-through sign combines two of the most proven sign types — the illuminated cabinet sign and the dimensional push-through letter — into a single integrated assembly. The result is a sign that offers the structural completeness and weather protection of a cabinet with the three-dimensional visual depth of push-through lettering. For many commercial applications, this combination represents the optimal balance of aesthetics, performance, and installation efficiency.

The construction begins with a fully welded aluminum cabinet that serves as both the structural backbone and the lighting enclosure. The cabinet face is a CNC-routed aluminum panel with precision letter cutouts, through which cast acrylic letters are pushed and sealed. Inside the cabinet, high-output LED modules mounted on aluminum rails wash light through the translucent acrylic letters, creating a brilliant backlit effect that is visible day and night.

Sunlite Signs manufactures cabinet push-through signs as complete, ready-to-install assemblies. Everything — cabinet, routed face, acrylic letters, LEDs, drivers, wiring, and mounting hardware — is built, assembled, tested, and crated under one roof. When the sign arrives at your job site, installation is a matter of mounting the cabinet, making electrical connections, and walking away. There is no field assembly of push-through components, no on-site letter seating, and no troubleshooting LED layouts in a lift bucket. This turnkey approach saves your crew hours of labor and eliminates field-assembly quality risks.

sections.heading: Cabinet Construction and Engineering Details
sections.content:
The cabinet is the foundation of every cabinet push-through sign, and Sunlite Signs builds cabinets to a standard that reflects our German engineering heritage. Every cabinet is fabricated from UL 48 compliant 0.080" aluminum sheet (heavier gauges available for oversized signs) using continuous MIG welds at all seams and joints. We do not use rivets, pop fasteners, or adhesive bonding on cabinet structures — only continuous welded construction provides the long-term structural integrity and weather resistance that outdoor sign applications demand.

Key cabinet engineering features include:

  • Internal LED Rails: Aluminum mounting rails are welded at calculated positions inside the cabinet to accept LED modules. Rail spacing is determined by photometric analysis for each specific letter layout.
  • Ventilation and Drainage: Screened ventilation openings at the top and bottom of the cabinet promote air circulation to manage internal temperatures. Weep holes at the lowest point allow any condensation to drain rather than accumulate around electrical components.
  • Access Panels: Every cabinet includes a hinged or removable service panel (typically at the rear or bottom) for access to LED modules, drivers, and wiring connections. Panels are gasketed to maintain weather protection while allowing tool-free access for maintenance.
  • Wiring Channels: Internal wire routing channels keep LED and power cables organized and protected from mechanical damage during shipping and installation.
  • Mounting Infrastructure: Welded mounting studs, cleat systems, or through-bolt patterns are integrated into the cabinet structure based on the installation method specified for the project.

Cabinet dimensions are custom-sized for each project. Standard depths range from 4" to 8", with custom depths available for specialized applications. Maximum single-piece cabinet dimensions are 60" x 144"; larger signs are fabricated as modular cabinet sections that bolt together seamlessly on site.

sections.heading: Advantages Over Separate Cabinet and Letter Installations
sections.content:
Sign shops often face a choice on storefront projects: install a separate sign cabinet and mount individual LED-illuminated channel letters or push-through components, or specify an integrated cabinet push-through sign. The integrated approach offers compelling advantages in most commercial applications.

Installation Efficiency: An integrated cabinet push-through sign is one assembly to mount. Compare this to installing a separate cabinet, then mounting individual letters or a push-through panel as a second step. The labor savings are significant — often 30-50% fewer installation hours compared to a two-component approach. Fewer hours on a lift or scaffolding means lower labor costs and less exposure to installation risks.

Consistent Illumination: Because the LED layout is engineered specifically for the letter positions on the routed face, illumination is perfectly optimized. Separate cabinet-and-letter installations sometimes suffer from uneven lighting because the letter placement doesn't align with the internal module grid — a problem that's impossible to fix without disassembling the sign.

Weather Performance: A single sealed cabinet assembly has fewer potential leak points than a multi-component installation. The push-through letters are sealed to the routed face before the sign leaves our facility, and the face is gasketed to the cabinet — creating a double-barrier weather protection system.

Simplified Permitting: Many jurisdictions issue permits based on the sign assembly as a unit. An integrated cabinet push-through sign is a single UL 48-listed assembly, which simplifies the permitting documentation compared to separate components that may each require individual listing documentation.

For sign shops, specifying cabinet push-through signs from Sunlite Signs means delivering a premium product with less field labor, fewer callbacks, and a streamlined permitting process. That combination directly improves project profitability.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a cabinet push-through sign and separate push-through letters?
faqs.answer: A cabinet push-through sign integrates the routed face panel, acrylic letters, LED system, and housing into one factory-assembled, UL 48-listed unit. Separate push-through letters are mounted onto existing surfaces or cabinets, requiring more field assembly and potentially compromising weather sealing.

faqs.question: What cabinet depths are available for push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Standard depths range from 4" to 8", with custom depths available. The optimal depth depends on letter size, LED module beam angles, and the desired illumination uniformity. Our engineering team calculates the right depth for each project.

faqs.question: How much installation time does a cabinet push-through sign save?
faqs.answer: Integrated cabinet push-through signs typically require 30-50% fewer installation hours compared to separate cabinet and letter installations. Everything arrives factory-assembled, tested, and ready to mount — no field assembly of push-through components is needed.

faqs.question: Can cabinet push-through signs be produced in modular sections?
faqs.answer: Yes. Signs exceeding our single-piece maximum of 60" x 144" are fabricated as modular cabinet sections that bolt together on site with seamless, weather-tight connections. Each section is individually tested before shipping.

faqs.question: How do you access LEDs and drivers inside a cabinet push-through sign?
faqs.answer: Every cabinet includes a gasketed hinged or removable service panel, typically at the rear or bottom. This provides tool-free access to LED modules, drivers, and wiring connections for maintenance without removing the sign from the wall.

title: Colored Acrylic Push-Through Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale colored acrylic push-through signs in any translucent tint. Brand-matched colors, even backlit illumination, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Translucent colored acrylic letters that transform white LED backlighting into vibrant brand-specific hues. Precision color matching for signs that glow in your client's exact palette.

sections.heading: How Colored Acrylic Creates Distinctive Backlit Effects
sections.content:
Colored acrylic push-through signs take the dimensional impact of standard push-through lettering and add another layer of brand differentiation: color — a premium option alongside front-lit channel letters. Instead of the classic white-glow-through-translucent-acrylic look, colored push-through letters filter the white LED backlighting through tinted acrylic, producing a vibrant, saturated glow in virtually any color. The effect is particularly striking at night, when the illuminated colored letters stand out dramatically against the painted aluminum panel.

The physics of colored acrylic illumination are straightforward but important to understand for proper sign specification. The acrylic acts as a color filter — it absorbs certain wavelengths of the white LED light and transmits others, producing the perceived color. This filtering process inherently reduces the total light output compared to white or clear acrylic, because a portion of the light energy is absorbed rather than transmitted. The degree of light reduction depends on the color: light tints like yellow and amber may transmit 60-70% of incident light, while deep colors like red and blue may transmit only 20-35%.

At Sunlite Signs, we account for this light transmission differential in our LED layout engineering. Signs with dark-colored acrylic receive a higher-density LED module grid to compensate for the lower transmittance, ensuring the finished sign appears bright and vibrant rather than dim. This attention to the optical engineering of colored push-through signs is what separates a well-built sign from one that disappoints after installation.

sections.heading: Color Options and Brand Matching
sections.content:
Sunlite Signs offers one of the most comprehensive colored acrylic selections — paired with our LED module technology for optimal illumination available for push-through sign fabrication. Our standard color inventory includes over 100 translucent colors from leading acrylic manufacturers including Plexiglas, Acrylite, and Chemcast. These range from subtle tints and pastels to deep saturated hues, and from standard primaries to specialty fluorescent and light-diffusing colors.

For brand-specific color matching, we offer several approaches:

  • Standard Color Match: We compare the client's PMS or RAL color specification against our translucent acrylic inventory and select the closest available match. For many brand colors, a close or exact match is available in stock sheet material.
  • Custom Cast Acrylic: For colors that are not available in standard inventory, we can source custom-cast acrylic sheets from our manufacturing partners. Custom casting requires larger minimum orders and longer lead times (typically 4-6 weeks for material procurement), but it achieves an exact color match.
  • Surface-Applied Color Film: For precise color matching on shorter timelines, we can apply translucent vinyl film to the face of clear or white acrylic. This method allows us to match virtually any PMS value using digitally printed film, and it is especially effective for multi-color logos or designs that require different colors in different areas.
  • Multi-Color Combinations: Signs that incorporate multiple acrylic colors — such as a logo with two or three distinct color areas — are fabricated with each color as a separate push-through element, precision-fitted into the same routed panel.

We provide physical color samples before production whenever a new or critical color match is involved. Our team will work with your shop to confirm the exact hue and light transmission characteristics before committing to production, ensuring the finished sign meets your client's brand expectations.

sections.heading: Design Considerations for Colored Push-Through Signs
sections.content:
Designing with colored acrylic in push-through applications requires understanding how color interacts with LED illumination, panel finishes, and the viewing environment. Sunlite Signs' engineering team consults on every colored push-through project to ensure the final result matches the designer's intent.

Key design considerations include:

  • Panel Color Contrast: The color of the aluminum panel dramatically affects the visual impact of colored push-through letters. Dark panels (black, charcoal, deep navy) create maximum contrast with brightly colored illuminated letters. Light panels provide a softer, more subtle effect. We recommend reviewing rendered visualizations before finalizing the panel color.
  • Daylight vs. Nighttime Appearance: Colored acrylic looks different when illuminated versus when viewed under ambient daylight. Deep reds and blues appear nearly opaque during the day but glow vividly at night. Lighter tints maintain more visual consistency between day and night presentations. We advise clients on these characteristics so there are no surprises after installation.
  • Color Consistency Across Units: For multi-location rollouts, color consistency is critical. We track acrylic lot numbers and specify material from the same production runs whenever possible. When lot matching is not feasible, we perform side-by-side transmittance testing to verify visual consistency before production.
  • LED Color Temperature Interaction: The color temperature of the LED modules affects how the colored acrylic appears when illuminated. Warm white LEDs (3000-4000K) shift the acrylic color toward warmer tones, while cool white (5000-6500K) produces a crisper, more true-to-material color. We recommend 5000K or 6500K LEDs for most colored acrylic applications to maintain color accuracy.

For projects where color fidelity is paramount, we offer an illuminated color proof — a sample acrylic swatch mounted over LED modules at the same density as the production sign, photographed under controlled conditions and shipped to your shop for approval. This extra step eliminates any uncertainty about the final illuminated appearance.

faqs.question: How many colored acrylic options are available for push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Our standard inventory includes over 100 translucent colors from tier-one manufacturers. For colors not available in stock, we can source custom-cast acrylic or apply digitally printed translucent film to achieve any PMS or RAL color match.

faqs.question: Does colored acrylic reduce the brightness of backlit push-through signs?
faqs.answer: Yes — colored acrylic transmits less light than white or clear acrylic because it absorbs certain wavelengths. We compensate by increasing LED module density in the sign layout, ensuring colored letters appear bright and vibrant rather than dim.

faqs.question: Can you match a specific PMS brand color in acrylic?
faqs.answer: In most cases, yes. We match PMS colors using standard translucent acrylic inventory, custom-cast sheets, or surface-applied translucent film. We provide physical color samples and can produce illuminated color proofs for critical brand applications.

faqs.question: What LED color temperature works best with colored acrylic?
faqs.answer: We recommend 5000K or 6500K cool white LEDs for most colored acrylic applications. Cool white light produces the most accurate color rendition through tinted acrylic. Warm white LEDs shift the perceived color toward warmer tones, which may not match brand specifications.

faqs.question: Can a single push-through sign include multiple acrylic colors?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Multi-color designs are fabricated with each color as a separate push-through element, precision-fitted into the same routed panel. This technique is common for logos and brand marks that incorporate two or more distinct colors.
